seoTitle: How to Import an HTML Email Template to Brevo (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to paste custom-coded HTML into Brevo's raw code editor, save it as a template, and test rendering across inboxes before sending.
intro: If you already have a custom-built HTML email design, you don't need to rebuild it in Brevo's drag-and-drop editor from scratch. This guide shows how to open the raw HTML code editor, paste in your markup, and save it as a reusable template. It also covers keeping image sources hosted remotely so your file size stays within delivery limits.

steps.heading: Start a new template
steps.body: Go to Campaigns, then Templates, and click New template to set up your tracking name, sender, and subject line.

steps.heading: Choose the HTML custom code option
steps.body: On the design selection screen, click Start from scratch and select the HTML custom code editor card instead of the standard drag-and-drop builder.

steps.heading: Copy your source markup
steps.body: Open your custom-built email file in a text editor on your computer and copy the entire HTML code.

steps.heading: Paste it into Brevo
steps.body: Return to the Brevo code editor panel and paste your copied source code directly into the main code window.

steps.heading: Save and test
steps.body: Save the layout as a template with a clear name, then use Preview & Test to check how it renders across device sizes before saving for future campaigns.

faq.question: Is there a file size limit for HTML templates in Brevo?
faq.answer: Yes, it's best practice to keep your total email package under 1MB by using remote image links rather than embedded files.

faq.question: Can I still edit my HTML template later using drag-and-drop blocks?
faq.answer: Custom HTML templates preserve your original coded structure, so editing is generally done by updating the raw code rather than switching to block-based editing.

faq.question: Why would I use HTML code instead of the visual builder?
faq.answer: Custom HTML lets you preserve advanced structural rules and responsive frameworks that go beyond what standard drag-and-drop blocks support.

faq.question: How do I check if my imported template displays correctly?
faq.answer: Use the Preview & Test feature to inspect how your code renders across major smartphone and desktop email clients before sending.
